comment_text.sn36.6:1.25: The ablative dukkhasmā here is explained by the commentary as taking an instrumental sense, like the preceding terms.
comment_text.sn36.6:4.1: The text says they “do not feel” the feeling, thus directly contradicting the main theme of the discourse. Bodhi inserts “mental” to resolve this. The commentary does not notice this verse at all, as it only comments on “learned” after “appraised” in the next verse, suggesting that either the verse was inserted after the commentary, or that it posed no interpretive difficulties. The initial line is also metrically imperfect: as a tuṭṭhubha, the final syllable of vedayati should be long; perhaps it was originally the reflexive vedayate. It seems to me the line is corrupt, and the simplest emendation is to assume a double negative that was later omitted; the omission of the negative is attested elsewhere in Pali verse (eg. Snp 3.8:17.4). Or perhaps, without emending, the initial na should be distributed to both noun and verb.

root_text.sn36.6:1.1: “Assutavā, bhikkhave, puthujjano sukhampi vedanaṁ vedayati, dukkhampi vedanaṁ vedayati, adukkhamasukhampi vedanaṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.2: Sutavā, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako sukhampi vedanaṁ vedayati, dukkhampi vedanaṁ vedayati, adukkhamasukhampi vedanaṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.3: Tatra, bhikkhave, ko viseso ko adhippayāso kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanenā”ti?
root_text.sn36.6:1.5: assutavā, bhikkhave, puthujjano dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno socati kilamati paridevati urattāḷiṁ kandati sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.10: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, puriso dvisallena vedanaṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.11: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno socati kilamati paridevati urattāḷiṁ kandati sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.14: Tassāyeva kho pana dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno paṭighavā hoti.
root_text.sn36.6:1.15: Tamenaṁ dukkhāya vedanāya paṭighavantaṁ, yo dukkhāya vedanāya paṭighānusayo, so anuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:1.16: So dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno kāmasukhaṁ abhinandati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.18: Na hi so, bhikkhave, pajānāti assutavā puthujjano aññatra kāmasukhā dukkhāya vedanāya nissaraṇaṁ,
root_text.sn36.6:1.19: tassa kāmasukhañca abhinandato, yo sukhāya vedanāya rāgānusayo, so anuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:1.20: So tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn36.6:1.21: Tassa tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ appajānato, yo adukkhamasukhāya vedanāya avijjānusayo, so anuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:1.22: So sukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, saññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.23: Dukkhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, saññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.24: Adukkhamasukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, saññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:1.25: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘assutavā puthujjano saññutto jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi, saññutto dukkhasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn36.6:2.1: Sutavā ca kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno na socati, na kilamati, na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.2: Tamenaṁ dutiyena sallena anuvedhaṁ na vijjheyya.
root_text.sn36.6:3.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, puriso ekasallena vedanaṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno na socati, na kilamati, na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.7: Tassāyeva kho pana dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno paṭighavā na hoti.
root_text.sn36.6:3.8: Tamenaṁ dukkhāya vedanāya appaṭighavantaṁ, yo dukkhāya vedanāya paṭighānusayo, so nānuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:3.9: So dukkhāya vedanāya phuṭṭho samāno kāmasukhaṁ nābhinandati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.11: Pajānāti hi so, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako aññatra kāmasukhā dukkhāya vedanāya nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn36.6:3.12: Tassa kāmasukhaṁ nābhinandato yo sukhāya vedanāya rāgānusayo, so nānuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:3.13: So tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavaṁ ca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn36.6:3.14: Tassa tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānato, yo adukkhamasukhāya vedanāya avijjānusayo, so nānuseti.
root_text.sn36.6:3.15: So sukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.16: Dukkhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.17: Adukkhamasukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaññutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn36.6:3.18: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘sutavā ariyasāvako visaññutto jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi, visaññutto dukkhasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn36.6:3.19: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso, ayaṁ adhippayāso, idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanenāti.

translation_text.sn36.6:1.1: “Mendicants, an unlearned ordinary person feels pleasant, painful, and neutral feelings.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.2: A learned noble disciple also feels pleasant, painful, and neutral feelings.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.3: What, then, is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an ordinary unlearned person?”
translation_text.sn36.6:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn36.6:1.5: “When an unlearned ordinary person experiences painful physical feelings they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.8: It’s like a person who is struck with an arrow,
translation_text.sn36.6:1.9: only to be struck with a second arrow.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.10: That person experiences the feeling of two arrows.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.11: In the same way, when an unlearned ordinary person experiences painful physical feelings they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.14: When they’re touched by painful feeling, they resist it.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.15: The underlying tendency for repulsion towards painful feeling underlies that.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.16: When touched by painful feeling they look forward to enjoying sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.18: Because an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t understand any escape from painful feeling apart from sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.19: Since they look forward to enjoying sensual pleasures, the underlying tendency to greed for pleasant feeling underlies that.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.20: They don’t truly understand feelings’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.21: The underlying tendency to ignorance about neutral feeling underlies that.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.22: If they feel a pleasant feeling, they feel it attached.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.23: If they feel a painful feeling, they feel it attached.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.24: If they feel a neutral feeling, they feel it attached.
translation_text.sn36.6:1.25: They’re called an unlearned ordinary person who is attached to rebirth, old age, and death, to sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress. They are attached to suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn36.6:2.1: When a learned noble disciple experiences painful physical feelings they don’t sorrow or wail or lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.1: It’s like a person who is struck with an arrow,
translation_text.sn36.6:3.2: but was not struck with a second arrow.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.3: That person would experience the feeling of one arrow.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.4: In the same way, when a learned noble disciple experiences painful physical feelings they don’t sorrow or wail or lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.7: When they’re touched by painful feeling, they don’t resist it.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.8: There’s no underlying tendency for repulsion towards painful feeling underlying that.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.9: When touched by painful feeling they don’t look forward to enjoying sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.11: Because a learned noble disciple understands an escape from painful feeling apart from sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.12: Since they don’t look forward to enjoying sensual pleasures, there’s no underlying tendency to greed for pleasant feeling underlying that.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.13: They truly understand feelings’ origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.14: There’s no underlying tendency to ignorance about neutral feeling underlying that.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.15: If they feel a pleasant feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.16: If they feel a painful feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.17: If they feel a neutral feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.18: They’re called a learned noble disciple who is detached from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress. They are detached from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn36.6:3.19: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person.
translation_text.sn36.6:4.1: A wise and learned person does not not feel
translation_text.sn36.6:4.2: the feeling of pleasure and pain.
translation_text.sn36.6:4.3: This is the great difference of skill
translation_text.sn36.6:4.4: between the wise one and the ordinary.
translation_text.sn36.6:5.1: A learned person who has appraised the teaching
translation_text.sn36.6:5.2: discerns this world and the next.
translation_text.sn36.6:5.3: Desirable things don’t disturb their mind,
translation_text.sn36.6:5.4: nor are they repelled by the undesirable.
translation_text.sn36.6:6.2: are cleared and ended, they are no more.
translation_text.sn36.6:6.4: they who have gone beyond rebirth <j>understand rightly.”

variant_text.sn36.6:1.3: adhippayāso → adhippāyo (bj, mr); adhippāyaso (sya-all, km); adhippāyoso (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn36.6:1.8: sallena vijjheyya → sallena vijjheyyuṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.sn36.6:1.9: sallena anuvedhaṁ vijjheyya → sallena anuvedhaṁ vijjheyyuṁ (bj, sya-all, km); sallena vijjheyyuṁ (pts1ed)
